activities_projects.title: Covalent Bonding in Molecules
activities_projects.objective: Understand how atoms share electrons to form bonds
steps: Draw the atomic structure of Hydrogen (1 electron) and Oxygen (6 valence electrons)
Show how two Hydrogen atoms share electrons with one Oxygen atom
Draw the electron-dot structure for Water ($H_2O$)
Circle the shared pairs of electrons
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that covalent bonds are formed by the sharing of electron pairs between atoms
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct representation of the electron-dot structure for water (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Versatile Nature of Carbon: Chain Making
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the property of catenation in carbon atoms
steps: Make small balls of clay to represent Carbon atoms
Use toothpicks to represent single bonds
Connect 4 carbon atoms in a straight line (Butane)
Try to rearrange the same 4 atoms into a branched structure (Isobutane)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies catenation and isomerism as reasons for the large number of carbon compounds
activities_projects.evaluation: Ability to demonstrate both a straight chain and a branched chain structure (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Saturated vs. Unsaturated Hydrocarbons
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between alkanes, alkenes, and alkynes
steps: Create a table with three columns: Alkane, Alkene, Alkyne
Write the general formula for each ($C_nH_{2n+2}$, $C_nH_{2n}$, $C_nH_{2n-2}$)
Draw the structure of Ethane, Ethene, and Ethyne
Label which ones have double or triple bonds
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates hydrocarbons based on the type of carbon-carbon bonds present
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of formulas and structural diagrams for the three types (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Allotropes of Carbon
activities_projects.objective: Compare the physical structures of Diamond and Graphite
steps: Observe the 'lead' of a pencil and note its slipperiness and color
Read about the 3D rigid structure of Diamond
Draw a simple sketch showing layers in Graphite vs. the tetrahedral mesh in Diamond
Explain why Graphite conducts electricity but Diamond does not
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that the same element can have different physical forms due to atom arrangement
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the link between structure and electrical conductivity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Homologous Series Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify the pattern in a group of carbon compounds
steps: Write the formulas for Methane, Ethane, Propane, and Butane
Calculate the difference in atoms between Methane ($CH_4$) and Ethane ($C_2H_6$)
Check if the difference is the same for the next pairs in the series
Identify the common functional group (if any) and the repeating unit ($-CH_2-$)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that members of a homologous series differ by a $-CH_2-$ unit
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the constant difference in molecular mass (14u) (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Testing for Carboxylic Acids
activities_projects.objective: Observe the reaction of Ethanoic acid with baking soda
steps: Take a small amount of baking soda in a glass
Add a few drops of vinegar to it
Observe the brisk effervescence
Discuss which gas is responsible for the bubbles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that carboxylic acids react with carbonates to release carbon dioxide gas
activities_projects.evaluation: Reporting the observation and naming the gas evolved (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Solubility of Alcohol
activities_projects.objective: Observe the physical property of Ethanol
steps: Take 5ml of water in a test tube
Add 2ml of Ethanol to the water
Shake the tube and observe if the two liquids mix
Note the smell and appearance of the resulting solution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that small-chain alcohols are completely miscible in water
activities_projects.evaluation: Describing the observed miscibility and the characteristic smell (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Combustion of Carbon Compounds
activities_projects.objective: Observe the flame produced by burning different fuels
steps: Light a candle and observe the color of the flame
Hold a clean metal plate over the yellow part of the flame
Observe if any black soot (carbon) deposits on the plate
Repeat with a small amount of camphor and compare the soot levels
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that incomplete combustion of saturated/unsaturated carbons produces soot
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate description of the flame color and soot formation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Esterification: The Fruity Smell
activities_projects.objective: Understand the formation of an ester
steps: Mix 1ml of ethanol and 1ml of ethanoic acid in a test tube
Add a few drops of conc. $H_2SO_4$ (catalyst)
Warm the tube in a water bath for 5 minutes
Pour the contents into a beaker of water and smell the mixture
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that esters are sweet-smelling substances used in perfumes and flavorings
activities_projects.evaluation: Description of the 'fruity' odor produced during the reaction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Soap vs. Detergent in Hard Water
activities_projects.objective: Observe the cleaning action of soap
materials: Hard water (or add Epsom salt to tap water)
steps: Take distilled water in one tube and hard water in the other
Add equal amounts of soap solution to both and shake vigorously
Compare the amount of foam (lather) formed
Observe if any white curd-like precipitate (scum) forms in the hard water
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that soaps are less effective in hard water due to scum formation
activities_projects.evaluation: Explaining why lather formation differs between the two water samples (10 marks)
